display_name: The Master Sculptor of the Gupta Age
one_line: An anonymous master sculptor of the Gupta era, forging the serene classical style of Sarnath, Udayagiri and Deogarh.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s, lean and wiry with craftsman’s muscles and a slightly stooped, work-hardened posture.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown North Indian complexion, straight nose, neatly trimmed moustache, faint stubble on chin, a thin vertical tilak of pale sandalwood on the forehead, soft but focused downcast eyes mirroring the Sarnath Buddha’s serenity, default expression calm and introspective with a hint of intense concentration.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair gathered into a compact topknot slightly behind the crown, sides and back neatly combed, a few loose strands over temples; a simple narrow white cotton headband tied around the forehead just below the hairline, knot at the back.
appearance.costume: A knee-length off-white cotton angavastra draped tightly over the left shoulder and across the torso leaving the right shoulder and arm mostly bare, the cloth’s edge outlined by a thin reddish-brown woven border; beneath it a plain, unstitched white cotton dhoti wrapped securely and tied at the waist, length reaching mid-calf with a narrow front pleat; a slim dark brown leather waist-belt over the dhoti to tuck small tools; bare chest except for a single tulsi bead mala worn close to the neck; feet in simple brown leather sandals with toe loop; minimal jewellery: a pair of small gold hoop earrings and a narrow copper bangle on the right wrist; lower legs dusted with stone powder, edges of garments subtly stained from quarry work.
appearance.signature: A slightly oversized iron hammer with a dark wooden handle worn smooth by use, its head wrapped with a thin strip of cloth for grip, held in the right hand, and a flat, broad-tipped iron chisel tucked horizontally into the belt at the front.

ref_desc: A lean, medium-brown Gupta-age sculptor with an oval face, topknot and white headband, wearing an off-white angavastra over a white dhoti with a reddish-brown border, small gold hoops, copper bangle, brown sandals, and carrying a dark-handled iron hammer with a chisel at his belt in warm off-white and brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a late-30s Gupta-era Indian male sculptor: lean, medium-brown skin, oval face, high cheekbones, straight nose, small moustache, faint chin stubble, soft downcast eyes with a calm, focused expression, a thin vertical pale sandalwood tilak on his forehead; thick black hair pulled into a compact topknot slightly behind the crown, sides neatly combed, a narrow white cotton headband tied around the forehead with the knot at the back and a few loose strands over the temples. He wears an off-white cotton angavastra draped over the left shoulder and diagonally across the torso, leaving the right shoulder and arm mostly bare, the cloth edged with a thin reddish-brown woven border, and a plain white cotton dhoti wrapped and tied at the waist, falling to mid-calf with a narrow front pleat, subtly stained with stone dust. Over the dhoti is a slim dark brown leather belt holding small tools, including a flat, broad-tipped iron chisel tucked horizontally at the front; his bare chest shows a single short tulsi bead mala around the neck. On his wrists is a narrow copper bangle on the right, and in his ears small gold hoop earrings; his feet wear simple brown leather sandals with a toe loop, with his lower legs lightly dusted with stone powder. His signature prop is a slightly oversized iron hammer with a dark wooden handle wrapped with cloth near the grip, held naturally in his right hand, with the chisel clearly visible at his belt. Use a warm, earthy palette of off-white, warm brown, dark brown, muted red-brown borders and bronze accents, and show front, side and back views to emphasize the consistent face, topknot with headband, angavastra drape, dhoti, and hammer-and-chisel tools.
